info.title: reeve
info.description: Fleet desired-state manager (Margo-inspired). Human API consumed by the reeve UI, plus the device-facing wire surface.

post.summary:
POST /api/auth/login (password mode only — 404 elsewhere: the surface
does not exist under proxy/none).

POST /api/auth/logout — deletes the session, clears the cookie.
Idempotent: no session is still a 204.

get.summary:
GET /api/auth/me — who am I, and what role am I acting with
(mode-aware: REEVE_AUTH=none reports anonymous acting as admin).

post.summary:
POST /api/auth/setup — first-boot admin creation (D1): valid only while
zero users exist and only with the one-time token logged at startup.
Creates the admin and logs them in.

POST /api/deploy (operator+) — deploy a stack to a scope (§11.4).
Authors `app.yaml` (package ref + `enabled:true` + profile) into the
scope's layer(s) via the tree authoring path; re-renders affected
devices.

403.description: Below operator role, or scope owned by another tier (federation §8.4)

422.description: Invalid stack, scope, or unknown device

get.summary: GET /api/devices (viewer+) — the fleet, ordered by device id.

200.description: All enrolled devices with presence, labels, layer-chain membership and current deployment states

get.summary:
GET /api/devices/{device_id} (viewer+) — one device, with render
provenance.

200.description: Device detail: summary plus render provenance (revision ids, manifest version, per-app secrets_version)

patch.summary:
PATCH /api/devices/{device_id} (operator+) — partial device update
(spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.3). Re-renders on any
fleet/site/type change; tag/displayName/pin changes do not re-render.

post.summary:
POST /api/devices/{device_id}/decommission (operator+) — revoke the
device credential(s) and tombstone the device so its desired state
stops being served (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.3). Idempotent.

get.summary:
GET /api/devices/{device_id}/journal (viewer+) — status journal
page, newest first (§7.3 forensic record; late-backfilled records
appear in seq order, not arrival order).

parameters.name: before_seq
parameters.description: Return records with seq strictly below this (older page)

get.summary:
GET /api/devices/{device_id}/logs?deployment=<id> (viewer+).
Lists stored log metas newest-first. `deployment` is required.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Deployment id to list logs for

get.summary: GET /api/devices/{device_id}/logs/{log_id} (viewer+).
get.description:
Returns the full log. Defaults to JSON `{ meta, text }`; a request
with `Accept: text/plain` gets the raw body as `text/plain`.

parameters.name: log_id
parameters.description: Log id from the list route

200.description: The log (JSON meta+text, or text/plain body)

404.description: No such log for this device

get.summary:
GET /api/durability/status — the §9.4 API surface (viewer+; the UI
renders "last verified restore" and the degraded flag from this).

200.description: Durability tier status: degraded flag, upload lag, last verified restore

get.summary:
GET /api/federation/status (viewer+): this tier's federation state
(§8.2 queryable). Root tiers report mode "root" and their delegated
children; gateways report upstream, sync bookkeeping and cursors.

GET /api/groups (viewer+) — the fleet->site containment tree.
`?fleet=<name>` (optionally `&kind=site`) scopes the read to one
fleet's children (lazy drill-down).

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Optional filter (`fleet`|`site`); with `fleet` requests that fleet's sites

parameters.name: fleet
parameters.description: Scoped-children read: only this fleet's subtree

200.description: The fleet->site containment tree (or one fleet's subtree)

post.summary:
POST /api/groups (operator+) — create a fleet or a site. A site
requires `parentId` = an existing fleet; a fleet must have none.
Duplicate name (a fleet globally, a site within its fleet) => 409.

409.description: Duplicate name (fleet globally, or site within its fleet)

422.description: Invalid name, or a site without/with a bad parent fleet, or a fleet with a parent

delete.summary:
DELETE /api/groups/{id} (operator+) — delete a group. Refused (409)
when it is still referenced by a device (or, for a fleet, still has
child sites): refuse is safer than a silent cascade that would orphan
live assignments — reassign/remove first.

409.description: In use — reassign devices (and remove child sites) first

patch.summary:
PATCH /api/groups/{id} (operator+) — rename a group. Refused (409) when
the group is still referenced by a device (or, for a fleet, still has
child sites): the device name columns stay valid because a live name is
never changed out from under them — reassign first. A rename that
collides with a sibling is also 409.

409.description: In use (reassign first), or duplicate name

get.summary:
GET /api/history[?limit=N] (viewer+) — the change log, newest first,
each with a human summary (§11.5).

GET /api/history/{id} (viewer+) — the plain diff: changed apps +
scopes, never raw layer paths (§11.5).

post.summary:
POST /api/history/{id}/undo (operator+) — author a NEW revision that
restores the whole tree to its content as of BEFORE change `{id}`
(§11.5 Undo). manifestVersion still only ever climbs (content may
revert; the version counter never does — §11.5 note). Only local
changes are undoable; upstream (synced) changes are refused.

200.description: Undone — a new change restored prior content

get.summary: GET /api/join-tokens (admin/operator) — hashes and metadata only.

200.description: All join tokens, newest first (hashes and metadata only)

post.summary:
POST /api/join-tokens (admin/operator, D4). Returns the raw token —
shown exactly once.

201.description: Join token created; raw token shown exactly once

404.description: Unknown device_id for a re-enroll token

delete.summary:
DELETE /api/join-tokens/{token_hash} (admin/operator) — revoke.
Idempotent: unknown or already-revoked is still 204.

parameters.name: token_hash
parameters.description: Hex sha256 of the join token

204.description: Revoked (idempotent: unknown or already-revoked is still 204)

get.summary:
GET /api/reeve/v1/capabilities (device-auth'd; anonymous pull of
anything device-facing is disabled by default, §10.2).

post.summary: POST /api/reeve/v1/devices/{device_id}/logs (DEVICE auth).
post.description:
The agent uploads its OWN captured compose output; the path
`device_id` MUST match the authenticated device token, else 403.
Body is a [`DeployLogUpload`] JSON; bodies over [`MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES`]
are rejected 413 by the `DefaultBodyLimit` layer on the route.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: The uploading device (must match the token)

200.description: Stored; returns the new log id

403.description: device_id does not match the token

post.summary: POST /api/reeve/v1/enroll (D4 step 1-2).

200.description: Enrolled (or idempotently resumed); the device credential is shown exactly once

401.description: Invalid, expired, or exhausted join token

get.summary: GET /api/reeve/v1/events (§6.1) — viewer+.
get.description:
Event payload schemas (the rev-003/1 table, §6.3) are registered
as OpenAPI components by `openapi.rs` (D10: the UI's invalidation
handlers consume generated event types); the stream itself is SSE
(`event:` = type name, `data:` = the JSON payload, `id:` = the
per-stream monotonic id).

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma-separated event-type filter (§6.1); unknown names ignored. Types: reset, device-presence, deployment-status, terminal-session, health-state, verify-restore, durability-lag, rollout, secret-rotation, federation-sync

parameters.name: Last-Event-ID
parameters.description: Resume after this event id (§6.2); when replay is impossible a `reset` event is sent first

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of cache-invalidation hints (§6.2: droppable, at-most-once)

post.summary:
POST /api/reeve/v1/journal/{deviceId} — backfill batch
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3). Replies [`JournalAck`]: the
highest contiguously ingested seq, which is what permits agent-side
journal eviction (§7.1).

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Device id (must match the device credential)

get.summary:
GET /api/reeve/v1/manifest — the device-scoped State Manifest
(§10.2). Renders on demand when the device's row is behind the
local head (fresh enrollment, missed pass), then serves the stored
manifest bytes verbatim so the ETag is exact.

parameters.name: if-none-match
parameters.description: RFC 9110 conditional GET against the manifest's strong ETag

200.description: The device's current State Manifest (ETag header set)

post.summary:
POST /api/reeve/v1/secrets/resolve (device auth; §12.3/§12.6).
The device receives exactly its own resolution: names resolve down
ITS chain; unknown/unscoped names are absent from the response.

200.description: The device's own resolution; unknown/unscoped names are simply absent

get.summary:
GET /api/reeve/v1/terminal/{device_id} — the UI leg (§5.1),
operator-initiated (§5.3). Every denial is audited (§5.4) and
answered BEFORE upgrade.

101.description: WebSocket upgrade: byte-transparent terminal bridge (xterm.js peer; spec/reeve/03-terminal.md §5)

403.description: Below operator role, terminal disabled by desired state, or unattributable auth mode

409.description: Device offline or session limit reached

post.summary:
POST /api/render — operator+ manual kick: re-render every device at
the current local head (C4). No-change devices are not bumped (D3).

get.summary: GET /api/rollouts (viewer+) — newest first.

post.summary:
POST /api/rollouts (operator+) — roll out the CURRENT desired config
to a scope in waves (REV-010 §11.5). The operator names a scope
(§11.4) + optional tag cohort; the server resolves the device set,
pins the current local head, and stages it.
post.description:
DECISION (baseline): un-advanced cohort devices are held at the head
revision's PARENT (the config just before this one). The authoring/
deploy commit has usually already rendered every device at head, so
creation re-pins cohort devices to that baseline; devices that polled
in the gap converge back to it. manifestVersion stays strictly
monotonic throughout (§11.5 — content may revert, versions never do).

422.description: No config to roll out, empty scope, or invalid waves/gate

get.summary:
GET /api/rollouts/{id} (viewer+) — full status: waves with live
per-device classification (§11.6: advanced / converged /
undetermined / failed), the pinned/unaffected count (§11.1 MUST),
recorded gate results, and the transition history.

post.summary: POST /api/rollouts/{id}/abort (operator+).

200.description: Aborted (pausing permanently — records and holds retained, §11.2)

post.summary: POST /api/rollouts/{id}/pause (operator+).

409.description: Not pausable from its current state

post.summary: POST /api/rollouts/{id}/resume (operator+).

409.description: Not resumable from its current state

post.summary:
POST /api/rollouts/{id}/rollback (operator+) — start a NEW rollout
returning the cohort to its pre-rollout config (REV-010 §11.5:
rollback is a new rollout of the previous config, NEVER "select
revision N"). The original rollout is aborted first (if still
active/paused) so its holds survive to be taken over; the rollback
advances the same cohort to the config the original held them at
before it ran. manifestVersion still only ever climbs (§11.5).

409.description: Cohort already targeted by another active rollout

get.summary:
GET /api/secrets (viewer+): METADATA ONLY — name, scope, version,
created/rotated. Values are never readable through any route, by
anyone (§12.2 write-only after entry).

200.description: All secrets' metadata, ordered by (name, scope) — never values

put.summary:
PUT /api/secrets (operator+; §12.2 write-only). Set or rotate:
an existing (name, scope) gets a version bump. Kicks a render pass
so affected devices' manifests pick up the new secrets_version
(§12.4 propagation).

200.description: Set or rotated; metadata only, never the value

delete.summary:
DELETE /api/secrets/{scope}/{name} (operator+). 404 when absent.
Kicks a render pass: referencing apps' secrets_version changes
(their references stop resolving), so consumers are notified.

get.summary:
GET /api/server — server version + compiled-in extension
advertisement + declared tier for the ops UI. Human (session) leg of
the device-auth'd /api/reeve/v1/capabilities; viewer+.

200.description: Server version, advertised extensions, and declared tier

get.summary: GET /api/tier-tokens (viewer+): metadata, never raw tokens.

200.description: All tier tokens, newest first (metadata only)

post.summary:
POST /api/tier-tokens (admin — §8.7 "issued like join tokens by
admin"). Returns the raw token ONCE.

200.description: Tier token created; raw token shown exactly once

get.summary:
GET /api/tree/blame/{*path} — every revision at which the path
changed, ascending (blame = SELECT, D13). `digest: null` marks a
removal.

200.description: Every revision at which the path changed, ascending

get.summary:
GET /api/tree/diff/{a}/{b} — manifest diff between two revisions,
computed on read (D13).

put.summary:
PUT /api/tree/layers/{layer} — apply one layer's complete content
(D14 batch semantics: the body IS the layer; a file absent from the
body is removed). Identical content => no new revision.

403.description: Below operator role, or layer owned by another tier (federation §8.4)

422.description: Invalid layer name, path, or base64

put.summary:
PUT /api/tree/packages/{name}/{version} — vendor a margo package
directory into the tree (D11: packages are vendored in v1, keeping
render pure). The upload is validated by the margo-package crate
BEFORE anything is committed: invalid packages produce 422 and no
revision.

403.description: Below operator role, or path owned by another tier

422.description: Invalid name/version, path, base64, or package validation failure

get.summary:
GET /api/tree/revisions[?limit=N] — revision history, both streams,
newest first.

200.description: Revision history, both streams, newest first

get.summary:
GET /api/tree/revisions/{id} — metadata + full manifest
(path -> blob digest) at that revision.

GET /api/tree/revisions/{id}/files/{*path} — raw file content at a
revision.

404.description: Unknown revision or no such path at it

post.summary:
POST /api/undeploy (operator+) — remove a stack from a scope (§11.4:
"the same call removing the app from the scope"). Removes the app dir
from the scope's layer(s); re-renders affected devices.

200.description: Undeployed (or unchanged — the app was not in the scope)

403.description: Below operator role, or scope owned by another tier (federation §8.4)

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/clients/{clientId}/deployments/{deploymentId}/status —
Margo live path. Response codes per `deployment-status.md`: 200
added/updated, 400 malformed, 401 unauthenticated (middleware),
403 wrong device, 422 semantic error.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Device id (must match the device credential)

get.summary: GET /healthz — liveness (operational contract, CLAUDE.md). No auth.

AppManifestEntry.description:
Per-app State Manifest entry. The agent diffs `secrets_version`
separately from the bundle digest: bundle unchanged +
`secrets_version` changed ⇒ re-resolve secrets and minimally
re-up, no bundle re-pull (spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12;
docs/decisions/agent.md).

appId.description:
Application id (matches the rendered app dir /
`ApplicationDeployment.spec.applicationId`).

deploymentId.description:
Deterministic deployment id of the rendered
`ApplicationDeployment` (docs/decisions/tree-render.md).

secrets_version.description:
Hash of resolved secret names+versions, never values
(spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12; docs/decisions/delivery.md D13).
Spelled `secrets_version` on the wire — the exact token used
normatively throughout spec/reeve/.

BlameEntry.description:
One `GET /api/tree/blame/{*path}` entry: a revision at which the
path changed. `digest: null` marks a removal.

BundleRef.description:
Render-bundle reference — shaped after Margo's
`DeploymentBundleRef` (`workload-management-api-1.0.0.yaml`):
mediaType, digest, advisory sizeBytes, content-addressable url.

digest.description:
`sha256:<hex>`; MUST be verified after pull — `sizeBytes` is
advisory only, never integrity.

url.description:
Content-addressed pull URL (native OCI /v2 routes,
docs/decisions/delivery.md D7).

CommitResponse.description:
Commit outcome of both PUT endpoints (`changed: false` = identical
content, no new revision — D14 idempotence). Package PUTs also
carry validation `warnings`.

stream.description: Always `local` — authoring only ever targets the local stream.

ComponentStatus.description:
`components[]` entry (`deployment-status.md` "Component
Attributes"; `ComponentStatus` in
`workload-management-api-1.0.0.yaml`). MUST contain one entry per
component of the referenced `ApplicationDeployment`.

CreateGroupRequest.description:
`POST /api/groups` body: create a fleet (no parent) or a site (parent =
an existing fleet's id).

parentId.description:
Required for a site (the containing fleet's id); MUST be absent for
a fleet.

device_id.description: Existing device to bind a re-enroll token to (D4).

fleet.description:
Optional enrollment pre-assignment (§11.3): the group the
enrolling device lands in and the tags it carries at first
contact.

CreateRequest.description:
POST /api/rollouts body (REV-010 §11.5): a rollout targets a SCOPE
(§11.4) narrowed by an optional tag cohort, resolved to a device set
at creation — NOT a revision id chosen by the operator. The current
desired config (the local head) is pinned server-side and rolled out
in waves. Waves: exactly one of `waves` (explicit partition),
`strategy` (e.g. `["1", "10%", "rest"]`), `waveCount`, or none
(single wave = whole cohort).

scope.description: The deploy scope (§11.4) this rollout targets.

tagCohort.description:
Optional tag cohort narrowing the scope (D12: tags select
cohorts, never configuration). All pairs must match a device's
tags for it to be included.

rollbackOf.description: Present when this rollout is a rollback of another (§11.5).

scopeDescription.description: Human scope phrasing (§11.5: describe scope in words).

waves.description: The wave partition (device ids per wave).

name.description: Unique child-tier name (e.g. the gateway hostname).

site.description: The child's site label — the layer family it owns (§8.4).

syncPrefixes.description:
Tree-path prefixes the child may sync; default
[`DEFAULT_SYNC_PREFIXES`].

ttlSecs.description: Optional TTL; absent => non-expiring (revocation is the lever).

CreatedJoinToken.description: `POST /api/join-tokens` body: the raw token, shown exactly once.

join_token.description: The raw join token (`rvj_<64 hex>`) — only the hash is stored.

CreatedTierToken.description: `POST /api/tier-tokens` body: the raw tier token, shown once.

token.description: The raw tier token — only the hash is stored.

DeployLogContent.description:
GET-one response: metadata + the full text. (The route MAY also
serve raw `text/plain`; this is the JSON shape the UI consumes.)

DeployLogList.description: GET-list response: metas newest-first for one (device, deployment).

DeployLogMeta.description:
Listing/reference metadata for one stored log — everything the UI
needs for a list row, minus the (potentially large) text body,
which is fetched on demand by `id`.

capturedAt.description: The device-captured RFC 3339 timestamp, preserved verbatim.

id.description: Opaque server-assigned log id (the handle for the GET-one route).

sizeBytes.description: Byte length of the stored text.

DeployLogOutcome.description:
What the converge attempt this log covers resulted in — mirrors the
agent's per-deployment outcome, independent of the Margo state.

DeployLogPhase.description: Which compose phase produced this output.

DeployLogUpload.description:
The agent's upload body: one captured compose run for one
deployment. `text` is the combined stdout+stderr the agent
captured; `truncated` is true when the agent already clipped it to
its own cap before sending (the server additionally rejects bodies
over its accept cap).

appId.description: The application id (Margo `applicationId`) for display grouping.

capturedAt.description:
When the agent captured this run (RFC 3339, device clock —
preserved verbatim like the journal's `observedAt`).

deploymentId.description:
The Margo deployment id this run converged (same value the
device reports in `DeploymentStatusManifest.deploymentId`).

exitCode.description:
Process exit code when known (`None` if the agent never got one
— e.g. the binary could not be spawned).

truncated.description: True if the agent clipped `text` before upload.

DeployResponse.description:
Response of both deploy and undeploy: the authoring outcome plus the
human framing (§11.5 — never a raw layer path).

changed.description: `false` => identical content, no new revision (D14 idempotence).

DeploymentStatus.description:
Overall deployment `status` (`deployment-status.md` "Status
Attributes"; an object, per the example manifest and the OpenAPI
schema — the attribute table's `[]status` type is contradicted by
both and is read as a spec typo).

DeploymentStatusEvent.description:
`deployment-status` (§6.3): an ingested manifest changed a
deployment's overall state. `state` is the Margo enum
(`margo::status::DeploymentState`).

DeploymentStatusManifest.description:
`DeploymentStatusManifest` request body
(`deployment-status.md` "Request Body Attributes"; posted to
`POST /api/v1/clients/{clientId}/deployments/{deploymentId}/status`).

deploymentId.description: UUID of the deployment specification, assigned by the WFM.

deviceId.description:
Required only when reporting on behalf of a child device
(`deployment-status.md`); full hierarchy path when applicable.

oneOf.description:
reeve additive extension object, rev-004/1
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3). A vanilla WFM ignores
it; all Margo-defined fields above are present and unchanged
(spec/reeve/01-framework.md §3.7 audit row REV-004).

DeviceClass.description:
§11.6 per-device rollout view: advanced / converged / healthy /
undetermined / failed. `Pending` = not yet advanced by its wave.

DeviceDeploymentState.description:
Current state of one deployment on a device
(`deployment_status_current` row).

observedAt.description: Device-assigned RFC 3339 timestamp (absent for vanilla reports).

seq.description: Journal seq of the report (absent for vanilla Margo reports).

oneOf.description: `null` when the device has never been rendered.

since.description: RFC 3339 — since when the device has been in `state`.

unaffected.description:
D12/§11.1: render materially unchanged by the rollout — counts
as converged, surfaced so green never silently means "nothing
deployed here".

class.description:
Retained V2 columns, dormant after the REV-010 taxonomy remap
(kept for compatibility; no longer feed the config chain).

displayName.description:
Human rename, distinct from `device_id` (§11.3); `null` => fall
back to hostname.

fleet.description:
Hierarchy-tier assignment (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.1:
all -> fleet? -> site? -> type? -> device), each nullable.

labels.description:
Free-form labels (docs/decisions/tree-render.md D12: labels
group and filter, never configure). Alias: `tags` (§11.2 — the
operator-facing name for the same column).

lastSeenAt.description: Unix seconds of last contact; `null` = never seen.

pinned.description:
Pin hold (§11.3): the device keeps its current desired config and
is excluded from new deploys/rollouts until unpinned.

stale.description:
Identity superseded by a newer enrollment from the same
hostname (docs/decisions/agent.md D4 wiped-box case).

tags.description:
Free-form key/value tags (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.2) —
the same data as `labels`, under the fleet-model name the UI
uses. Ad-hoc grouping/filtering/rollout cohorts only; never
selects config (that is the layer chain's job).

tierOrigin.description:
Child tier this device reached us through (federation §8.3);
`null` = enrolled here.

DiffEntry.description:
One `GET /api/tree/diff/{a}/{b}` entry. `change` decides which
digests are present: `added` carries `new`, `removed` carries
`old`, `modified` carries both.

DiskSample.description:
Disk usage/free for one filesystem (spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md
§7.2). Inner field names are reeve-chosen (the spec pins the
sample's semantics, not its sub-shape); extensible like the rest
of the sample.

DurabilityLagEvent.description:
`durability-lag` (§6.3): changeset upload lag crossed/cleared a
threshold — ops dashboard signal (spec/reeve/07-durability.md
§9.3: changesets are keyed by generation id + monotonic seq).

generation.description:
Snapshot generation id (spec/reeve/07-durability.md §9.2 —
generation keys are string-shaped, e.g. `<rfc3339>-<schema>`).

lastSeq.description: Last uploaded changeset sequence within the generation.

DurabilityStatus.description:
The queryable status shape (§9.2 degraded flag, §9.3 upload lag,
§9.4 "last verified restore"). A deployment whose verify-restore
has never succeeded reads as having NO durability tier (§9.4) —
`effective_tier` encodes exactly that rule for the UI.

epoch.description:
Current server epoch (`settings.server_epoch`, spec/reeve/07-durability.md
§9.5): the high 16 bits of every manifestVersion. Restore fencing
increments it — the ops UI surfaces it next to durability posture.

DurabilityStatusResponse.description:
`GET /api/durability/status` body: [`DurabilityStatus`] plus the
§9.4 `effective_tier` (an unverified tier reads as none).

EnrollRequest.description:
Request body of `POST /api/reeve/v1/enroll` (D4 step 1):
`{ join_token, hostname, arch, agent_version }`.

hostname.description:
The enrolling box's hostname — the idempotency key for a retried
install with the same join token (D4).

join_token.description:
Operator-created join token (D4: TTL + max-uses, stored hashed
server-side). A re-enroll token is the same grammar, bound to an
existing device_id server-side.

EnrollResponse.description:
Response of `POST /api/reeve/v1/enroll` (D4 step 2): the issued
identity and the ONE device credential for every device-facing
surface (docs/decisions/auth.md D1).

device_token.description:
Enrollment-issued bearer token (`rvd_<64 hex>`). Shown exactly
once; the server keeps only its hash.

resumed.description:
True when this enrollment resumed an existing device identity
(idempotent re-run of the same join token + hostname, or a
re-enroll token bound to an existing device — D4).

ErrorBody.description:
The one error-body shape every JSON error response uses:
`{ "error": "<human-readable message>" }`. Schema component for the
D10 openapi.json (docs/decisions/ui.md); handlers keep building the
body via `json!` — this type documents the contract.

FederationStatus.description:
`GET /api/federation/status` body. `mode` decides which fields are
present: `gateway` carries the upstream/sync bookkeeping, `root`
only `childTiers` and `upstreamOriginHead`.

upstreamLocalRow.description: Local row id of the upstream head.

upstreamOriginHead.description: Parent-tier revision id of the synced upstream head.

FederationSyncEvent.description:
Federation sync outcome (`federation-sync` payload;
spec/reeve/06-federation.md §8.2 — sync state is queryable AND
evented; an id/digest divergence is surfaced here too, never
auto-resolved). Additive to the rev-003/1 table: unknown event
types are ignored by older clients (§6.3).

originHead.description: Parent-tier revision id of the synced upstream head, when known.

upstream.description: The parent tier URL this gateway syncs from.

FleetNode.description: One fleet with its contained sites.

sites.description: Sites contained by this fleet, ordered by name.

undeterminedAllowance.description:
Max undetermined devices a passing gate tolerates. Absent =
the whole wave may be undetermined (chronically-offline fleets,
Law 5); 0 = strict.

GroupKind.description: A group kind (§11.1): only fleet and site are contained groups.

parentId.description: The parent fleet's id for a site; `null` for a fleet.

GroupTree.description:
`GET /api/groups` body: the fleet->site containment tree. A scoped
read (`?fleet=<name>`) returns the same shape with only that fleet's
subtree in `fleets`.

HealthKind.description:
What is degraded (spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.4):
`Device` — the device itself breaches thresholds; `Link` — the
path was down but backfill shows the device was fine.

HealthSample.description:
A point-in-time health sample (spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md
§7.2): disk usage/free per relevant filesystem, memory usage,
load averages, per-workload container restart counts, agent
version, clock skew vs the server. "Fields are extensible;
receivers MUST ignore unknown sample fields" — unknown fields are
captured (and re-emitted) via `extra`.

clockSkewMs.description:
Clock skew versus the server in milliseconds, measured
opportunistically when connected (§7.2 — skew matters because
original timestamps are device-assigned).

disk.description: Per-filesystem usage, keyed by mount point / identifier.

restarts.description:
Per-workload container restart counts, from the active
Provider.

additionalProperties.description:
Extensible sample fields (§7.2): preserved verbatim so the
payload round-trips without loss.

HealthState.description:
Server-side health classification
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.4). `Unknown` (offline window
not yet backfilled) MUST be surfaced as unknown, never silently
assumed healthy or dead.

HistoryChange.description: One changed app+scope in a [`HistoryDetail`] (never a raw path).

changes.description:
Changed apps + scopes. Empty when the change touched only
non-app content (a vendored package) — see `otherChanges`.

otherChanges.description:
Count of changed tree paths outside any `apps/<app>/` (package
vendoring, etc.) — surfaced as a number, never as paths.

id.description:
Opaque change id (the revision id under the hood, but the UI
treats it as a change handle — §11.5).

summary.description:
Human one-liner, e.g. `deployed nginx to Site plant-a`, else
`config change`.

JoinTokenInfo.description: One join token row, as listed to operators (never the raw token).

device_id.description: Set => re-enroll token bound to this existing device (D4).

fleet.description:
Enrollment pre-assignment applied to the device that enrolls with
this token (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.3): hierarchy tiers
and free-form tags.

JournalAck.description:
Response: "the server replies with the highest contiguously
ingested sequence number; that acknowledgement is what permits
journal eviction" (spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3).

JournalBatch.description:
Request body of `POST /api/reeve/v1/journal/{deviceId}`
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3): unacknowledged records in
batches ordered by sequence number.

payload.description: The journaled payload; absent for gap marks.

nextBeforeSeq.description:
Pass as `before_seq` to fetch the next (older) page; absent
when this page reached the start of the journal.

JournalRecord.description:
One journal record in a backfill batch
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3 backfill path). Idempotency
key is `(deviceId, seq)` — deviceId travels in the endpoint path.

payload.description:
The journaled payload: a full `DeploymentStatusManifest` for
`status`, a [`HealthSample`] for `health`, a free-form object
for `lifecycle`, absent for `gap`.

JournalRecordKind.description:
Kind of a journal record (spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.1):
status reports, health samples, agent lifecycle marks (start,
converge begin/end, provider errors), and gap marks (forced
eviction of unacknowledged records — so the server can
distinguish "evicted" from "never happened").

LogIdResponse.description: `POST .../logs` response body: the new log id.

ManifestVersion.description:
`manifestVersion` — logically the pair `(epoch, counter)` compared
lexicographically, encoded on the wire as ONE monotonically
increasing unsigned 64-bit integer: epoch in the high 16 bits,
counter in the low 48 (spec/reeve/08-packaging.md §10.2). Plain
integer comparison IS the pair comparison, and the wire value
stays exactly Margo's modeled shape (`ManifestVersion`: monotonic
u64 in `[1, 2^64-1]`, `workload-management-api-1.0.0.yaml` —
"The first manifest MUST use 1").

Agent rules (§10.2): a non-increasing value is rejected and logged
as a SECURITY event; an increase that bumps the epoch bits is
accepted and logged as a NOTABLE event (a restore happened,
spec/reeve/07-durability.md §9.5 restore fencing).

displayName.description: Human rename; `null` clears it (falls back to hostname).

fleet.description:
Hierarchy assignment; `null` unassigns (removes the layer from
the chain). A change re-renders the device.

pinned.description:
Pin hold (§11.3). Needs no re-render (the device holds where it
is; unpinning is picked up on the next poll).

tags.description: Replace the free-form tag set (§11.2). `null` clears all tags.

since.description:
Unix seconds: channel-open time when online via channel,
last contact otherwise; `null` = never seen.

PutFilesRequest.description:
Body of both PUT endpoints: the complete content of one layer (or
package) as relative path -> base64(content). Base64 keeps the wire
JSON binary-safe (compose files are text, but package resources —
icons — are not).

files.description: Relative path within the layer/package -> standard-base64 bytes.

message.description: Commit message; a default is derived when absent.

PutSecretRequest.description:
Body of PUT /api/secrets. `value` is plaintext in RAM only — it is
sealed before touching the DB and never echoed back or logged.

ReeveStatusExtension.description:
The additive `reeve` object on a `DeploymentStatusManifest`
(spec/reeve/05-health-journal.md §7.3, rev-001 example):

```json
"reeve": { "observedAt": "2026-07-10T06:12:03Z", "seq": 48211,
           "health": { "...": "..." } }
```

The server uses `observedAt`/`seq` to place the report in history
and detect records it already holds. A vanilla WFM ignores the
whole object (spec/reeve/01-framework.md §3.7 audit row REV-004).

observedAt.description:
Original timestamp (RFC 3339) — assigned when journaled on
the device, never rewritten (§7 "original timestamp").

rendered.description: Devices whose manifest changed (and was bumped).

apps.description:
Per-app manifest entries — carries each app's `secrets_version`
(spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12.4).

bundleDigest.description: OCI image manifest digest the device pulls; `null` = zero apps.

contentDigest.description: sha256 over the rendered apps/** file set (change detector).

epoch.description:
High 16 bits — bumped by restore fencing
(spec/reeve/07-durability.md §9.5).

etag.description: Strong ETag of the served manifest JSON.

renderedRevision.description: Local-stream revision id this device was last rendered against.

ResetEvent.description:
`reset` (§6.2): replay from `Last-Event-ID` not possible; the
client MUST treat all cached state as stale and refetch.

ResolvedSecret.description:
One resolved secret: plaintext value + its version (the rotation
counter that feeds the manifest-level `secrets_version` hash,
spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12.4).

version.description: Version at resolution time — audit metadata, safe to log.

files.description: Full manifest at that revision: tree path -> blob digest.

RevisionInfo.description: Revision metadata as served to clients.

Role.description:
Human role (docs/decisions/auth.md D1): admin | operator | viewer.

Ordered so `Viewer < Operator < Admin`; "at least operator" is
`role >= Role::Operator`.

cohort.description:
The cohort spec as recorded at creation (`{scope, tagCohort,
description}`).

pinnedUnaffected.description: §11.1 MUST: devices whose render the rollout does not change.

wave.description:
Wave index within the rollout's ordered wave list
(spec/reeve/09-rollouts.md).

scopeDescription.description: Human scope phrasing (§11.5), e.g. `Site plant-a tagged env=prod`.

Scope.description:
A deploy/rollout target (§11.4). `kind` is the JSON discriminator:
`{"kind":"all"}`, `{"kind":"fleet","name":"north"}`, …,
`{"kind":"devices","ids":["dev-1","dev-2"]}`.

SecretInfo.description:
One secret's METADATA — the only readable surface after entry
(§12.2 write-only: name, scope, version, timestamps; never values).

SecretRotationEvent.description:
`secret-rotation` (§6.3): a secret version changed / all affected
devices report converged. Carries metadata only, never values
(§6.4; spec/reeve/10-secrets.md).

SecretRotationState.description:
Secret rotation propagation state (`secret-rotation` payload,
§6.3; spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12).

SecretWritten.description:
`PUT /api/secrets` body: metadata of the written secret — never
the value (§12.2 write-only).

version.description: Version after the write (bumped on rotation).

SecretsResolveRequest.description:
Request body: the secret names referenced (`${secret:<name>}`)
by the requesting device's rendered apps. Names only — never
values; names are metadata (spec/reeve/10-secrets.md §12.6
"who resolved what version when — metadata, not values").

SecretsResolveResponse.description:
Response body: resolutions keyed by requested name. A name the
device may not read — or that does not exist — is simply ABSENT
(indistinguishable by design: the resolve endpoint must not be a
secret-existence oracle beyond the device's own scope, §12.6).

ServerCapabilities.description:
The server-side advertisement at
`GET /api/reeve/v1/capabilities` — "its extension list plus
server version" (spec/reeve/01-framework.md §3.3). 404 or any
error means "vanilla Margo server"; the agent proceeds with pure
Margo behavior.

extensions.description: Same `"rev-NNN/V"` grammar as the agent side.

allOf.description:
Same fields as the device-auth'd
`/api/reeve/v1/capabilities` (server version + extensions).

site.description: The site label this instance serves; `null` on a root tier.

upstream.description:
Present iff this instance syncs from a parent tier
(`REEVE_UPSTREAM` set) — the operative federation signal (D9).

ServerInfo.description:
`GET /api/server` body: capability advertisement plus this
instance's declared topology tier (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md
§11.6) for the UI.

SiteNode.description: One site under a fleet in the [`GroupTree`].

StackRef.description:
The stack to deploy: a vendored package (`packages/<package>/
<version>/`, D11) and an optional deployment profile. `name` is the
app's dir under the scope layer (`apps/<name>/`) — defaults to the
package name, so `{package, version}` is the minimal stack.

name.description: App id in the layer (`apps/<name>/`). Absent => the package name.

profile.description:
Deployment profile id (Margo `deploymentProfiles[].id`); absent =>
the package's sole/only-compose profile is selected at render.

StateManifest.description:
The reeve State Manifest body returned by
`GET /api/reeve/v1/manifest` (spec/reeve/08-packaging.md §10.2) —
State-Manifest-shaped after Margo's `UnsignedAppStateManifest`
(`workload-management-api-1.0.0.yaml`): `manifestVersion`,
render-bundle digest + pull URL, per-app `secrets_version`.

oneOf.description:
Render-bundle reference. Follows Margo's `DeploymentBundleRef`
null rule: with zero apps the property MUST be present with
the value `null`, never omitted — so it is always serialized.

StatusError.description:
`error` element (`deployment-status.md` "Error Attributes").
Reserved gateway-generated codes: 101 unknown child device, 102
child device unreachable, 103 autonomous placement not supported.

source.description:
Device id (full hierarchy) or component/deployment name that
generated the error (`deployment-status.md`).

TerminalPhase.description:
Terminal session lifecycle phase (`terminal-session` payload,
§6.3; spec/reeve/03-terminal.md §5.4).

TerminalSessionEvent.description:
`terminal-session` (§6.3): session lifecycle transition. Exposes
session METADATA only (who, which device, when) — intentional
audit visibility; MUST NOT ever carry session content (§6.4).

revoked.description: False when the name was unknown or already revoked.

restoredTo.description:
The change id the content was restored to (before the undone
change); `null` when the undone change was the first.

revision.description: The new change id that restored prior content.

snapshotTs.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the snapshot that was verified.

VerifyRestoreOutcome.description:
Outcome of a verify-restore run (`verify-restore` payload, §6.3;
spec/reeve/07-durability.md §9.4).

VerifySummary.description:
Latest recorded run, for the status surface ("last verified
restore: <when>", §9.4).

WhoAmI.description:
`GET /api/auth/me` body. `kind` decides which identity fields are
present: `human` carries `user`+`role`, `device` carries
`deviceId`, `anonymous` carries neither.

oneOf.description:
The role this identity ACTS with (mode-aware: REEVE_AUTH=none
maps anonymous to admin); `null` = no access.

tags.name: tree
tags.description: Layered deployment tree: authoring, history, diff, blame, render

tags.name: deploy
tags.description: Deploy/undeploy a stack to a scope (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.4)

tags.name: devices
tags.description: Device fleet: presence, deployment states, render provenance, journal

tags.name: groups
tags.description: Location groups: the fleet->site containment tree (spec/reeve/11-fleet-model.md §11.1)

tags.name: federation
tags.description: Tier tokens and federation sync status (spec/reeve/06-federation.md)

tags.name: device
tags.description: Device-facing wire surface (enroll, manifest poll, status ingest)
